meta_title: For Freelancers: API integration guide to Solve no design skills (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to API integration guide and overcome no design skills. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: API Integration Guide for Non-Designers
content.seo_description: Master API integration to bypass design hurdles. A beginner-friendly guide for freelancers using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship professional apps fast.
content.answer_block: Stop struggling with CSS frameworks. By leveraging Antigravity to scaffold UI components and Claude Code to handle backend API orchestration, you can deliver pixel-perfect, data-driven applications without writing a single line of custom design code. This workflow automates the heavy lifting, allowing you to focus purely on high-value business logic.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking on your project deadline, and the client is waiting for a polished interface. You have the logic, but the visual execution feels like an insurmountable wall—you're a developer, not a pixel-pusher. The 'no design skills' trap has stalled more freelance careers than any technical bug ever could.

But here is the secret: you don't need a degree in UI/UX to ship premium software. By treating your front-end as a predictable API-driven stream, you can automate the entire assembly process. We are moving from manual styling to algorithmic construction, utilizing AI-native tools to turn raw data into high-conversion interfaces in minutes, not days.

steps.title: Define the Data Contract
steps.description: Before touching code, define your API requirements. Use an OpenAPI schema to ensure the frontend knows exactly what to render.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust OpenAPI 3.0 schema for a SaaS dashboard fetching user activity logs. Include fields for timestamp, action, and status.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create an openapi.yaml file based on the user activity schema'

steps.title: Scaffold the Interface
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate a clean, responsive shell that binds to your schema, bypassing manual CSS work.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React layout with Tailwind CSS that consumes an OpenAPI schema. Use a professional, minimalist 'SaaS dark mode' aesthetic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a React component structure using the schema in openapi.yaml'

steps.title: Automate API Client Generation
steps.description: Don't write fetch requests manually. Use your schema to generate type-safe API clients.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an Axios-based API client helper that maps all endpoints from the OpenAPI spec to typed function calls.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a typesafe API client using the openapi.yaml file'

steps.title: Connect Data to UI
steps.description: Map the API responses directly to the scaffolded components using React hooks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a custom hook 'useActivityLogs' that calls the API client and provides loading, error, and data states for the UI.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the useActivityLogs hook and connect it to the activity-table component'

steps.title: Refine and Ship
steps.description: Use Claude Code to perform a final audit of the accessibility and performance of the generated code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current codebase for accessibility compliance (WCAG) and suggest optimizations for faster rendering.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Audit the current project for accessibility and suggest performance improvements'

pro_tips: Always generate your UI from an OpenAPI spec; it acts as the 'source of truth' that prevents desync between backend and frontend.
Use CSS variables for your branding color palette so you can change the entire app's look by modifying only one file.
When using Claude Code, always ask it to explain the 'why' behind its architectural choices to level up your own knowledge.
Leverage mock services like MSW (Mock Service Worker) during development so you can build the UI even if the backend isn't ready.
Keep your components 'dumb'—pass data via props from a container component to ensure your UI remains reusable and easy to test.
content.next_steps: Once the integration is live, deploy to Vercel or Netlify using Claude Code's automated build scripts to see your work in production within minutes.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting design limitations hold your freelance business back. With this workflow, you can deliver high-end enterprise software consistently. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
